source_item.title: Armory first built a counter-drone system in six months, and now it has a Rs 100 crore defence order

slide1_hook.label: 2-Year-Old Startup Lands ₹100 Crore Defence Order
slide1_hook.subhead: India's youngest defence-tech bet just paid off big.

slide2_backstory.label: THE BACKSTORY
slide2_backstory.headline: A Border Crisis Nobody Solved Fast Enough
slide2_backstory.body: Drones were sneaking across India's borders to smuggle weapons and drugs. In 2024, IIT Bombay grad Amardeep Singh founded Armory to stop them.

slide3_news.label: THE UPDATE
slide3_news.headline: ₹100 Crore MoD Order for SURGE
slide3_news.body: Armory just won three MoD contracts worth ₹100 crore (~$10.5 Mn) for its SURGE counter-drone system. SURGE spots drones up to 5 km away and jams them up to 3 km.

slide4_why.label: WHY IT MATTERS
slide4_why.headline: What This Means for Defence Startups
bullets: MoD is betting on startups, not just old giants
Next: hard-kill drone tech and a new fundraise

slide5_engage.label: YOUR TAKE
slide5_engage.headline: Should the Ministry of Defence fast-track startups like Armory, or is moving fast on national security too risky?
slide5_engage.cta: Follow for more on India's biggest business moments, told simply.
